mod3._comment: Claude Code channel client. Start with: claude --dangerously-load-development-channels server:mod3. The env block pulls the harness session id into MOD3_SESSION_ID so each Claude Code session registers a distinct seat (instead of all collapsing into 'main').
mod3._keying_note: This binds each seat to a harness session_id (one seat per Claude Code session). The substrate-canonical model (per channel-as-substrate-primitive + seat-as-synchronous-coordination-surface) is one seat per (channel, identity) with N harnesses attaching to it — keyed by identity, not by harness. That requires a SeatReconciler with output arbitration across attached harnesses. Tracked as Wave 6d / future ADR. Session-keying is the transitional form.
mod3._followup: FIXME: promote the implicit harness↔seat relationship encoded by this env-pass to a first-class HarnessBinding Reconcilable record per substrate-bindings-as-k8s-rbac, so the substrate has authoritative visibility into 'which Claude Code session_id maps to which seat'.

_toolManifest.note: Tool manifest for documentation; capabilities and tools are declared by clients/channel_client.py.

inbound.description: Messages from the dashboard arrive as channel notifications

tools.name: mod3_dashboard_post
tools.description: Send text to the mod3 dashboard chat panel.

tools.name: mod3_speak
tools.description: Synthesize text to speech and play it through the Mod3 daemon.
